summary: End-to-end testing of PED.RESUS (post-restructure, no paywall). Backend: 9/9 pytest cases passed — root /api/ returns PED.RESUS message, /api/status POST+GET persistence works, and all /api/payments/* endpoints correctly return 404. Frontend: 50/52 assertions passed via Playwright; the 2 'failures' were test-script false positives (wrong mode id and substring search) and were confirmed working on re-test. All 12 tabs render, TopBar weight control is reactive, Resuscitation has 4 sub-buttons, Fluids no longer contains any Local Anaesthetic block, Sedation & Analgesia has 4 sub-tabs with all 8 nerve blocks and NYSORA/baby-blocks references, Severity Scores has 6 sub-sections with 3 pain tools, Clinical Pathways has both Interactive & Differential modes and PDF-export button appears on pathway results, Imaging has 4 modalities with Radiopaedia links, and the TopBar Export PDF button downloads `ped-emergency-{weight}kg.pdf` with no paywall.

issues: Inline images sourced from upload.wikimedia.org render as broken/placeholder icons in the preview environment. Per the spec this is acceptable because alt text and Radiopaedia link still render, but consider bundling local fallbacks or using onError swap to the 'View case on Radiopaedia' tile for a cleaner look.

critical_code_review_comments: backend/server.py uses deprecated FastAPI @app.on_event('shutdown'); migrate to lifespan handlers for FastAPI 0.110+ compatibility.

frontend/src/data/fluids.js still exports LOCAL_ANAESTHETICS but it is not imported anywhere (sedationAnalgesia.js defines its own). Dead export — remove to avoid confusion and keep the single source of truth in data/sedationAnalgesia.js.

frontend/src/App.js imports React icons individually; the TABS array is fine but TabsList wraps to multiple rows on narrow screens — consider horizontal scroll with scroll-snap for better mobile ergonomics (design-polish, not a bug).

CORS allow_origins defaults to '*' while allow_credentials=True — this combination is silently rejected by browsers. Since there is no auth, it's harmless for now, but set explicit origins once auth is added.
seed_data_creation: One TEST_<uuid> status check inserted via POST /api/status for persistence test; safe to leave.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — run with `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<preview-url> pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v`. Covers: root branding, status POST+GET roundtrip, and asserts /api/payments/* all return 404 (paywall removed). For Playwright selectors: Clinical Pathways mode ids are 'interactive' and 'differential' (singular); Sedation nerve-block testids are `block-<slug>` where slug is fascia-iliaca/digital/auricular/supraorbital/infraorbital/femoral/haematoma/penile; Pathway runner exposes `pathway-export-pdf` on result nodes. TopBar Export PDF issues a real download named `ped-emergency-{weight}kg.pdf` — watch for expect_download rather than a modal.
rca of the issue: No app bugs. Two initial Playwright 'failures' were self-inflicted: (a) mode id 'differentials' vs actual 'differential'; (b) nerve-block names checked against active-section inner_text instead of counting `block-<id>` buttons. Re-tested with correct selectors — all 8 nerve blocks and both pathway modes render and function as expected.
